title: The War Has Ended And Capital Will Rotate Out Of US Assets
gist: Paywall teaser: Anatole Kaletsky argues the Iran-Israel-US war that began February 28 has ended with a ceasefire signed by JD Vance and Iran's Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, removing the 'false prices' fog of war; the title signals a thesis that capital will now rotate out of US assets. Light card — the teaser stops before the argument is developed.

themes.theme: End of war removes price distortion

insights.claim: A ceasefire deal in the Iran-Israel-US war (begun February 28) was signed by US vice president JD Vance and Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, which Kaletsky argues ends the period of wartime 'false prices' across financial markets.
insights.speaker: Anatole Kaletsky
insights.so_what: If wartime distortion is lifting, rational pricing returns and positioning can normalize — the setup for a regime shift in asset allocation.
insights.quote: every financial market in the world was possibly trading at “false prices,” making rational investment analysis very challenging. This situation has now completely changed.

insights.claim: Kaletsky's stated thesis (per the title) is that with the war ended, capital will rotate out of US assets.
insights.speaker: Anatole Kaletsky
insights.so_what: A call to underweight US assets on a post-ceasefire rotation — the trade is the rotation itself; teaser does not yet show the mechanism.

insights.claim: Most media and market commentators dismissed the ceasefire as little more than an 'agreement to disagree' with root causes unresolved, a consensus view Kaletsky implicitly pushes back against.
insights.speaker: Anatole Kaletsky
insights.so_what: Positions against consensus skepticism on the truce — a contrarian read that the ceasefire is more durable/consequential than priced.
insights.quote: has been dismissed by most media and market commentators as little more than an “agreement to disagree,” with all the important questions unanswered
